entities.name: Regional Container Lines Public Company Limited

financial.note: Holds ~25.20% stake; Ngow Hock provides strategic backing and long-term stability for RCL's regional dominance.
Holds ~12.98% stake; indicative of significant foreign capital flows and sentiment towards the Thai logistics sector.

financial.entity: Bank of New York Mellon / State Street
financial.note: Hold combined ~2-3% stakes; reflects global institutional trust in RCL's operational transparency and dividend reliability.

supply_chain.note: Primary shipyard for RCL's 2026 fleet expansion, including two new 14,000 TEU eco-friendly vessels to meet IMO standards.
Fuel is the largest variable cost; 2026 focus on 'B24 VLSFO' biofuel to mitigate carbon taxes and rising crude prices.
Revenue is 100% dependent on global trade volume; performance is sensitive to the 2026 US-China trade 'tariff war' escalation.

equity_cross_holdings.note: The group's international operating hub; manages the deep-sea fleet and high-capacity regional routes.
Critical for the 2026 'Integrated Logistics' pivot to stabilize earnings against volatile ocean freight rates.

competitors.note: Primary rival in the Intra-Asia segment; both companies compete fiercely for slot capacity and feeder network dominance.
Rivalry in the spot-market-heavy routes; both stocks exhibit high volatility correlated with the SCFI (Shanghai Containerized Freight Index).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The most direct leading indicator for RCL's revenue; 2026 oversupply risks (10%+ surplus) act as a downward pressure on this index.
As a global carrier with USD-denominated freight income, a weaker THB significantly boosts translated net profits for SET reporting.
The return to Suez routing in early 2026 increased effective global capacity by ~3%, contributing to the current freight rate normalization.
